title: Polybotrya quercifolia
taxonomy: Polybotrya quercifolia is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Central and South America. As a member of the family Dryopteridaceae, Polybotrya quercifolia belongs to the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which consists of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Polybotrya quercifolia is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales.

intro: Polybotrya quercifolia, also known as the oak fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate, oak-like fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Polybotrya quercifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polybotrya quercifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions, or as a tincture, which is used to concentrate its active compounds.
